TimENC 2

TimENC

The Tauri UI now follows the old Python app more closely: separate pages, stronger defaults and clearer workflow.

Still not full 1.x parity, but this is now a real multi-page app flow instead of a placeholder page.

Encrypt

Encrypt a file or folder into a .timenc file with password and optional keyfile.

Ready.

Encryption Workflow

Like the old app, this page guides input, output, password and keyfile in one place and auto-suggests the target path.

Directory inputs become encrypted archives automatically. You can also drop a file or folder onto the window.
Enter a password.
With a keyfile, both password and keyfile are required later.
Suggested OutputWaiting for input path.
ProtectionChaCha20-Poly1305 with Argon2id and optional keyfile material.
Safer RestoreTraversal attacks are now rejected in both filenames and archives.

Decryption Workflow

This mirrors the old decrypt page: encrypted input, output folder, password and optional keyfile, with suggested restore location.

Suggested FolderWaiting for .timenc path.
CompatibilityHeader Argon2 parameters are now respected during decryption.
Restore SafetyRecovered files stay inside the chosen output directory.

Keyfile Tools

The old app surfaced keyfile generation directly in the main workflow. This page keeps that fast path explicit.

Generates a new 32-byte random keyfile. Existing files are not overwritten.

Last Result

Instead of transient text only, the latest operation stays visible here.

No operation run yet.

Current UI State

Compared with 1.x, the Tauri frontend now has much better workflow parity, but not yet full feature parity.

Now PresentNavigation, guided forms, password visibility, password strength and output auto-suggestion.
Still MissingNative file pickers, drag-and-drop, multilingual settings and richer notifications.
WhyThe repo still uses a static HTML frontend rather than a full web app stack.

Language

Choose the interface language. The selection is stored locally and applied on the next render immediately.

Updates

Check the latest GitHub release for TimENC.
